title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Go Client Systems · Execution Trace: Percent Encoding Query
summary: A release mirror accepts a configurable upstream URL and must prove the canonical origin, DNS answer, certificate name, and connected destination before download. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go percent encoding query increment in a Go 1.26 net/http destination-resolution and origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Percent Encoding Query · Theory Percent Encoding Query
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model receives base https://api4.example.test/v1/items/ and reference ../reports?q=a%2Fb. Resolve it structurally, compare origin and authority, trace two DNS answers, and reject any connected destination outside the approved range. Competency probe: Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
